parsed_review.summary: CP00-360 is a descriptor-only RP11.P07 failure-hermes-slice pack (risk B, 40 units, RP11.P07.M07.S05-RP11.P07.M09.S02) covering the M07 Test/Golden failure tail (18 rows), the full M08 Hermes Evidence Packet failure cycle (20 rows), and the M09 Claude Review Packet head (2 rows). Independent source inspection confirms the pack-level claim is well supported: pack identity, deliverable distribution (22/4/3/4/2/4/1) and micro-title distribution (18/20/2) are consistent across the registry binding, contract projection, manifest, and the live closeout-pack-plan.json, with deviation_from_plan=false. The implementation is genuinely descriptor-only: freezeCp360Result sets ~50 no-write/no-real-data flags to false, and every failure-row guard asserted by validateTimeExpenseCoreCp360FailureHermesSliceDescriptor (cross-tenant deny, no permission/audit-hint/blocked-claim/lock-token/fixture/hermes-body leaks, no retry/rollback/lock/test/integration runtime, no Hermes runtime receipt, Claude not final approver, human escalation required) is backed by concrete inherited row-extras. Validators and tests are meaningful (not stubbed) and bind to the live plan; the contract validator deepEquals the contract JSON against source-generated descriptors, requirements, and no-write attestation. The authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, human final approval required before runtime opening (CP00-361). Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic descriptor receipt/evidence shapes (..._no_write), never product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is satisfied: the forbidden_review_evidence list rejects auth failures, 0-byte output, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing fields, and the readiness auth preflight passed with valid non-zero JSON. production_ready is correctly false (status implemented_pending_review) with post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) appropriately absent and not falsely claimed. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 informational note: the reviewer-aid changed-file-scope.json double-lists the pack's own docs dir and a handoff note as both untracked implementation files and unrelated-preserved files; this is cosmetic and non-blocking (the authoritative manifest categorizes them correctly and no active implementation files are omitted). Claude is acting strictly as a read-only independent reviewer; final approval and the enterprise trust claim remain with human authority outside this receipt.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-categorizes pack docs dir and handoff note as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-360/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138) of the reviewer-aid scope file. These categories are intended to be mutually exclusive: the pack's own docs directory is legitimately a pack implementation/evidence artifact, while the manifest's authoritative worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved correctly excludes both files. The overlap is a scope-generator cosmetic quirk only; active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no active implementation file is improperly dropped, and the 8 active_pack_implementation_refs (the real deliverables) are unaffected. No impact on descriptor-only boundaries, no-write/no-real-data guarantees, validation, or the production_ready posture.
findings.recommendation: In the scope-file generator, treat the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/ directory and the active session handoff as pack-scoped untracked files only and exclude them from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, mirroring the manifest's categorization. Non-blocking for CP00-360 closeout.
